title: Adjective Order — A Beautiful Old Red Wooden Box
description: Learn the correct order for using multiple adjectives before a noun — one of the most important rules for sounding natural in English.

content.explanation:
In English, when we use MORE than one adjective before a noun, they must go in a specific order. Native speakers naturally follow this order without thinking about it.

🔴 WRONG ORDER (sounds very unnatural):
• a wooden old big brown box ❌

✅ CORRECT ORDER (sounds completely natural):
• a big old brown wooden box ✅

📋 THE ADJECTIVE ORDER RULE:
1. OPINION/GENERAL → beautiful, lovely, awful, interesting, strange
2. SIZE → big, small, tall, tiny, huge
3. AGE → old, young, new, ancient, modern
4. SHAPE → round, square, flat, oval
5. COLOUR → red, blue, dark, pale
6. ORIGIN → Indian, French, Chinese, South Indian
7. MATERIAL → wooden, cotton, leather, plastic, gold
8. PURPOSE → running (shoes), sleeping (bag), dining (table)

🎯 EASY MEMORY TIP: 'OSASCOMP'
Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose
examples: a lovely small old square gold ring
a beautiful long red silk saree
a big new black leather bag
a tiny ancient round silver coin
content.tip: You don't usually use more than 2-3 adjectives together in normal speech. But when you do, the order must be right!

content.explanation:
Let's see adjective order in real, everyday situations:

🛍️ SHOPPING / DESCRIBING OBJECTS:
• a beautiful blue cotton kurta (opinion → colour → material)
• a small new silver laptop (size → age → colour)
• a large old wooden dining table (size → age → material → purpose)

🏠 DESCRIBING PLACES:
• a lovely small cosy flat (opinion → size → opinion)
• a huge modern glass building (size → age → material)

👤 DESCRIBING PEOPLE:
• a tall young Indian woman (size → age → origin)
• a little old man (size → age)

⚠️ COMMA RULE:
• Adjectives of the SAME type need a comma between them:
  → a dark, stormy night (two opinions)
  → a tall, slim woman (two sizes)
• Adjectives of DIFFERENT types do NOT need a comma:
  → a beautiful young woman (no comma)
examples: She was wearing a gorgeous long green silk saree.
He lives in a small old stone house in the hills.
They bought a beautiful large round wooden dining table.
I found a tiny old brass coin at the market.
content.tip: Same adjective type = comma. Different adjective types = no comma.

vocabulary.meaning: decorated with a lot of detailed patterns
vocabulary.example: She bought an ornate silver jewellery box.
My grandmother has an antique wooden clock.
He has a sleek new black laptop.

vocabulary.meaning: decorated with patterns sewn in thread
vocabulary.example: She wore a beautiful embroidered cotton top.

vocabulary.meaning: made by hand, not by machine
vocabulary.example: I bought a lovely handmade clay pot from the market.

vocabulary.meaning: strong and solid; not likely to break
vocabulary.example: We need a big sturdy wooden table for the garden.
Please handle the small glass figurine carefully — it's fragile.
The jewellery had intricate gold patterns.

content.instruction: Listen and repeat. Notice how the adjectives flow naturally in the correct order.

items.text: She was wearing a beautiful long red silk saree.
They have a big old brown leather sofa.
He bought a small new silver watch.
It's a lovely little antique wooden box.
We visited a beautiful ancient stone temple.

tasks.question: Which sentence has the adjectives in the CORRECT order?
options: a wooden old big round table
a big old round wooden table
a round old wooden big table
a big wooden round old table
tasks.correct_answer: a big old round wooden table
tasks.explanation: Correct order: SIZE (big) → AGE (old) → SHAPE (round) → MATERIAL (wooden). This follows the OSASCOMP rule.

tasks.question: Which phrase is in the CORRECT adjective order?
options: a cotton beautiful long blue saree
a beautiful long blue cotton saree
a long blue beautiful cotton saree
a blue cotton beautiful long saree
tasks.correct_answer: a beautiful long blue cotton saree
tasks.explanation: Correct order: OPINION (beautiful) → SIZE (long) → COLOUR (blue) → MATERIAL (cotton). Opinion always comes first.

tasks.question: Meera found an old coin at an antique market. It is small, round, gold, and ancient. What is the CORRECT description?
options: a gold small round ancient coin
an ancient round gold small coin
a small ancient round gold coin
a round small gold ancient coin
tasks.correct_answer: a small ancient round gold coin
tasks.explanation: Correct order: SIZE (small) → AGE (ancient) → SHAPE (round) → COLOUR/MATERIAL (gold). SIZE comes before AGE.

tasks.question: Which sentence correctly describes a kurta?
options: He wore a cotton white long beautiful kurta.
He wore a beautiful long white cotton kurta.
He wore a long beautiful cotton white kurta.
He wore a white beautiful long cotton kurta.
tasks.correct_answer: He wore a beautiful long white cotton kurta.
tasks.explanation: OPINION (beautiful) → SIZE (long) → COLOUR (white) → MATERIAL (cotton). This is the correct adjective order.

tasks.instruction: Put the adjectives in the correct order to complete the sentence.

items.sentence: She carries a _____ bag everywhere. (leather / small / black / lovely)
We sat around a _____ table. (round / dining / large / teak)
He has a _____ phone. (new / sleek / Chinese / black / small)

tasks.instruction: Correct the adjective order in each sentence.

items.sentence: She was wearing a silk red long beautiful saree.
items.answer: She was wearing a beautiful long red silk saree.

items.sentence: We visited an stone ancient beautiful temple.
items.answer: We visited a beautiful ancient stone temple.

items.sentence: He bought a leather brown big new bag.
items.answer: He bought a big new brown leather bag.

tasks.instruction: Rearrange the words to make a sentence with correct adjective order.

items.answer: My grandmother has a beautiful old brass lamp.
They sell lovely small handmade clay pots at the market.

tasks.instruction: Rearrange the words to make a sentence with correct adjective order.

items.answer: She found a tiny ancient round gold coin.
We stayed in a beautiful small old stone guesthouse.

tasks.instruction: Match the scrambled adjective group with the correct ordered version.

pairs.right: a beautiful long blue cotton saree
a large old round wooden table
a tiny antique round gold pendant
a sleek new black Italian motorbike
a beautiful huge ancient stone fort

tasks.title: Shopping at a Craft Market — Jaipur

dialogue.context: Priya and her cousin Arun are shopping at a traditional craft market in Jaipur. They are describing and deciding what to buy.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Arun, look at this! Isn't it gorgeous?

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: Oh wow — what is it exactly?

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: It's a beautiful large brass elephant — a traditional Rajasthani one. I think it would look perfect in our living room.

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: It's stunning. And look at those intricate patterns on it! What about this one next to it?

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: That's a small old silver mirror — probably antique. I love the ornate round frame.

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: It's lovely. But it might be too fragile. What about the fabric section? I want to find something for Amma.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Good idea! Oh, Arun — look at this beautiful long red silk dupatta. Amma would love it.

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: It's perfect. And it's not expensive at all. What about this blue one?

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: That's a lovely deep blue embroidered cotton dupatta — also very nice. But I prefer the red silk one.

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: Let's get both — they're both beautiful. Oh! Look at this box over here.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: That's a gorgeous little antique wooden jewellery box. The carving is so detailed!

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: How much is it? It looks like it could be expensive.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Only ₹800. For a beautiful handmade old Rajasthani wooden box? That's a bargain!

turns.speaker: Arun
turns.text: Let's buy everything then! This market is incredible.

comprehension_questions.question: What does Priya want to buy for the living room?
options: A small silver mirror
A beautiful large brass elephant
A red silk dupatta
A wooden jewellery box

comprehension_questions.question: Which dupatta does Priya prefer?
options: The blue cotton one
The embroidered one
The beautiful long red silk one
The small antique one
comprehension_questions.correct_answer: The beautiful long red silk one

comprehension_questions.question: How much does the wooden jewellery box cost?

tasks.instruction: Find the adjective order mistake and write the correct version.

items.sentence: She wore a silk blue long beautiful saree to the wedding.
items.explanation: Correct order: Opinion (beautiful) → Size (long) → Colour (blue) → Material (silk).

items.sentence: He drives a German black new big car.
items.explanation: Correct order: Size (big) → Age (new) → Colour (black) → Origin (German).

items.sentence: They bought a round wooden old lovely table.
items.explanation: Correct order: Opinion (lovely) → Age (old) → Shape (round) → Material (wooden).

tasks.scenario: You want to describe a gift you received — a necklace that is old, beautiful, made of gold, and shaped like a small leaf. How do you describe it correctly?
tasks.question: Which is the correct description?
options: It's a gold beautiful small old leaf-shaped necklace.
It's a small old gold beautiful leaf-shaped necklace.
It's a beautiful small old gold leaf-shaped necklace.
It's a leaf-shaped beautiful old small gold necklace.
tasks.correct_answer: It's a beautiful small old gold leaf-shaped necklace.
tasks.explanation: Opinion (beautiful) → Size (small) → Age (old) → Colour/Material (gold) → Shape/Purpose (leaf-shaped). This follows the correct OSASCOMP order.

tasks.instruction: Describe 4-5 objects in your home using at least 2 adjectives for each one. Make sure your adjectives are in the correct order. Speak for at least 60 seconds.
tasks.prompt: Describe objects in your home using multiple adjectives in the correct order.
tasks.example_response: Let me describe some things in my home. First, we have a large old dark wooden dining table in our main room — my grandfather bought it over 40 years ago and it's still very sturdy. Next to it are six small round plastic chairs. In the corner, there is a beautiful tall brass lamp that my mother brought from a temple shop in Tirupati. In my bedroom, I have a comfortable large new cotton mattress that I bought last year. On my desk, there is a sleek new black plastic laptop that I use for work. My favourite object is a tiny ancient gold ring that belonged to my great-grandmother — it has a beautiful round stone in the middle. And finally, we have a lovely old heavy iron key that we use as a paperweight — we don't even know which door it belongs to!

tasks.instruction: You are at a craft market or textile shop. Describe 4-5 things you see using multiple adjectives in the correct order. You can describe clothes, jewellery, furniture, or handicrafts. Speak for at least 60 seconds.
tasks.prompt: Describe items at a craft market using correct adjective order.
tasks.example_response: I'm at a beautiful craft market in Mysore and there are so many amazing things here. First, I can see a large round brass tray with intricate flower patterns carved into it — it looks like traditional South Indian design. Next to it is a small old silver oil lamp — the kind you use for puja at home. On the fabric stall, there is a gorgeous long green silk Mysore silk saree — it's shimmering in the sunlight! There are also some beautiful small colourful cotton bags that are handmade by local women's groups. I can see a lovely old wooden dancing Ganesha statue that is quite large — maybe 30 centimetres tall. And on the last stall, there are some fascinating tiny ancient brass temple bells that make a beautiful sound when you ring them. Everything here is so unique and represents the incredible craftsmanship of Karnataka.

tasks.instruction: Put the adjectives in the correct order to complete this description of a traditional Indian home.
tasks.paragraph: My ancestral home in Kerala is a wonderful place. It is a (1: large / old / beautiful / Kerala-style) _____ house with high ceilings and wooden beams. The main hall has a (2: brass / antique / heavy / round) _____ lamp hanging from the ceiling. In the prayer room, there is a (3: small / gold / beautiful / carved) _____ idol of Ganesha. The kitchen has a (4: large / round / stone / old) _____ grinding stone that my grandmother still uses every morning. On the veranda, there are (5: wooden / rocking / lovely / two / old) _____ chairs where the elders sit in the evenings. The walls are decorated with (6: intricate / old / circular / brass) _____ mirrors. It is one of the most beautiful places I have ever seen.
tasks.hint: Remember OSASCOMP: Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose

what_you_learned: Adjective order rule: Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose
Same-type adjectives need a comma; different-type adjectives do not
Never use more than 3-4 adjectives in one phrase in normal speech
Common patterns: opinion + size + colour + material / opinion + age + material
Key vocab: ornate, antique, sleek, embroidered, handmade, sturdy, fragile, intricate
content.grammar_summary: When using multiple adjectives before a noun, follow OSASCOMP order: Opinion, Size, Age, Shape, Colour, Origin, Material, Purpose. This makes English sound natural and fluent.
content.encouragement: Brilliant! Adjective order is one of the most noticeable markers of advanced English. You now know the rule that native speakers follow instinctively. Keep practising! 🏆
